info.title: Easy Genomics API
info.description:
Internal REST API for the Easy Genomics platform.
All routes require a Cognito JWT bearer token unless marked with security: [].

servers.description: Easy Genomics API (placeholder — replaced by CDK integration)

ErrorCode.description: EG-1xx: generic, EG-2xx: organization, EG-3xx: laboratory, EG-4xx: user, EG-5xx: AWS HealthOmics, EG-6xx: Seqera Cloud

cacheHit.description: Set to true if Amazon Web Services HealthOmics found a matching entry in the run cache for this task.

cacheS3Uri.description: The S3 URI of the cache location.

gpus.description: The number of Graphics Processing Units (GPU) specified for the task.

instanceType.description: The instance type for a task.

progress.description: Aggregated task progress derived from HealthOmics ListRunTasks.

TagIds.description: Standard (non-workflow, non-permanent, non-batch) tags on this sample.

BatchTagId.description: At most one batch tag per sample.

ContentsSummary.description: Human-readable summary for list UI, e.g. "2 files · R1 + R2".

WorkflowName.description: Optional: omitted if the run was created without a WorkflowName.

RunCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the laboratory run's `CreatedAt`.

InputFileCount.description: Total number of input files recorded for this run at creation time.

InputFileKeys.description: Full list of S3 object keys (lab-scoped) for the run, used by the tooltip's "select samples" action.

ExpiresAt.description:
Mirror of the laboratory run's `ExpiresAt` (epoch seconds, DynamoDB TTL) at the time this
usage entry was recorded or last refreshed. Undefined when the run is non-expiring
(`Laboratory.RunRetentionMonths === 0`) or when the run had not yet reached a terminal
status. Surfaced to the front-end so the sequence collections page can power the
"Expiring soon" scope filter without an extra round trip to the run table.

Kind.description: Omitted or `standard` for legacy tag rows.

Platform.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. Identifies the platform that ran the workflow.

WorkflowExternalId.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. HealthOmics workflowId or Seqera pipelineId.

WorkflowVersionName.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. Empty string represents the default version.

TagIds.description: Standard (non-workflow, non-permanent, non-batch) tags on this sample.

BatchTagId.description: At most one batch tag per sample.

ContentsSummary.description: Human-readable summary for list UI, e.g. "2 files · R1 + R2".

WorkflowName.description: Optional: omitted if the run was created without a WorkflowName.

RunCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the laboratory run's `CreatedAt`.

InputFileCount.description: Total number of input files recorded for this run at creation time.

InputFileKeys.description: Full list of S3 object keys (lab-scoped) for the run, used by the tooltip's "select samples" action.

Kind.description: Omitted or `standard` for legacy tag rows.

Platform.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. Identifies the platform that ran the workflow.

WorkflowExternalId.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. HealthOmics workflowId or Seqera pipelineId.

WorkflowVersionName.description: Set on workflow-kind tags. Empty string represents the default version.

BatchTagId.description: At most one batch tag id if the file is assigned to a batch.

WorkflowTagIds.description: Workflow tag ids that have been associated with this file via run launches.

IsPermanent.description:
True when the file carries the laboratory's system-managed `permanent` tag. Files marked
permanent are never auto-deleted by the run-retention cleanup job (see
`process-expired-laboratory-data` Lambda).

LaboratoryRunUsages.description:
Per-laboratory-run usage history for this file, sorted newest first by `RunCreatedAt`.
Each entry records that the file appeared in a run's `InputFileKeys` at run creation time,
regardless of whether the run had a `WorkflowExternalId` (so non-workflow-tagged runs are
still represented). Empty / omitted when the file has never been used in a run.

WorkflowName.description: Optional: omitted if the run was created without a WorkflowName.

RunCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the laboratory run's `CreatedAt`.

InputFileCount.description: Total number of input files recorded for this run at creation time.

InputFileKeys.description: Full list of S3 object keys (lab-scoped) for the run, used by the tooltip's "select samples" action.

ExpiresAt.description:
Mirror of the laboratory run's `ExpiresAt` (epoch seconds, DynamoDB TTL) at the time this
usage entry was recorded or last refreshed. Undefined when the run is non-expiring
(`Laboratory.RunRetentionMonths === 0`) or when the run had not yet reached a terminal
status. Surfaced to the front-end so the sequence collections page can power the
"Expiring soon" scope filter without an extra round trip to the run table.

WorkflowName.description: Optional: omitted if the run was created without a WorkflowName.

RunCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the laboratory run's `CreatedAt`.

InputFileCount.description: Total number of input files recorded for this run at creation time.

InputFileKeys.description: Full list of S3 object keys (lab-scoped) for the run, used by the tooltip's "select samples" action.

ExpiresAt.description:
Mirror of the laboratory run's `ExpiresAt` (epoch seconds, DynamoDB TTL) at the time this
usage entry was recorded or last refreshed. Undefined when the run is non-expiring
(`Laboratory.RunRetentionMonths === 0`) or when the run had not yet reached a terminal
status. Surfaced to the front-end so the sequence collections page can power the
"Expiring soon" scope filter without an extra round trip to the run table.

AwsHealthOmicsNetworkingMode.description:
VPC networking mode for this lab's HealthOmics runs. Omitted ⇒ RESTRICTED
(S3 + ECR, same Region only). VPC routes runs through the referenced,
ops-created HealthOmics Configuration named by AwsHealthOmicsVpcConfigurationName.
Independent of AwsHealthOmicsEnabled — a lab may hold a dormant VPC config
while HealthOmics is disabled.

AwsHealthOmicsVpcConfigurationName.description: Name of an existing, ACTIVE HealthOmics Configuration resource, created out-of-band by ops.

EnableNewWorkflowsByDefault.description:
When true, workflows/pipelines without a DENY row are allowed (new AWS-console workflows included).
When false/omitted, only explicit ALLOW rows grant access.

RunRetentionMonths.description:
Laboratory-wide run retention policy, in months, applied after a run reaches a terminal state.
- 0 means "never delete run records" (no TTL expiration).

HealthOmicsLlmProvider.description:
BYOK LLM provider selection per integration. Each lab can pick a different
provider/model/key for HealthOmics vs Seqera. Setting a provider IS the
enable signal — when set, ambiguous HealthOmics failures and free-text
Seqera errors are routed to the configured LLM. The deterministic
HealthOmics lookup table runs regardless. Bedrock uses the platform Lambda
IAM; OpenAI / Anthropic read the lab's own API key from SSM at classify
time.

SSM paths for the API keys:
  `/easy-genomics/organization/{OrganizationId}/laboratory/{LaboratoryId}/llm-api-key-healthomics`
  `/easy-genomics/organization/{OrganizationId}/laboratory/{LaboratoryId}/llm-api-key-seqera`

HealthOmicsLogEnrichmentEnabled.description:
When true, the failure classifier fetches the failed HealthOmics run's
CloudWatch engine log, redacts PII + secrets, and sends a bounded excerpt to
the configured LLM for deeper diagnosis. Requires a HealthOmics LLM provider.

HasHealthOmicsLlmApiKey.description: Boolean indicators returned by read-laboratory; the actual keys never leave SSM.
Boolean indicators. The actual keys live in SSM and are never returned.

WorkflowExternalId.description:
Platform-side workflow identifier (HealthOmics workflowId or Seqera pipelineId).
Persisted at run-creation time so the data tagging system can associate inputs
with a stable workflow identity.

InputFileKeys.description:
S3 object keys (within the laboratory bucket) that were used as inputs
for this run. Populated at run-creation time and consumed by the data
tagging system to record file -> workflow associations. Optional because
legacy runs and runs whose input keys could not be determined will not
have this field.

TerminalAt.description:
ISO timestamp indicating when the run first reached a terminal state.
Used as the anchor for retention/TTL recomputation.

ExpiresAt.description:
DynamoDB TTL epoch timestamp (in seconds).
When enabled, DynamoDB will remove items after this timestamp.

RunDurationSeconds.description:
Actual execution duration reported by the underlying platform, in seconds.
Populated by the status-check processor from Seqera `workflow.duration`
or AWS HealthOmics `stopTime - startTime`.

FailureReason.description:
Top-level failure reason reported by the platform when a run reaches FAILED state.
Sourced from HealthOmics `failureReason` or Seqera `workflow.errorMessage`.
Absent on runs that failed before this field was introduced.

FailureStatusMessage.description:
HealthOmics human-readable `statusMessage` (often carries the failing task name
and a CloudWatch log link). Kept separate from the machine-code `FailureReason`
so the classifier receives both signals.

FailureErrorReport.description:
Seqera `workflow.errorReport` — the richer Nextflow stack-trace / error detail,
distinct from the one-line `workflow.errorMessage` stored in `FailureReason`.

FailureOwner.description:
Party responsible for resolving a failure: Lab user (input/data), Bioinformatician
(workflow definition), AWS (transient, retry), or Ambiguous (needs investigation).
Populated asynchronously by the classification pipeline after a FAILED transition.

FailureSummary.description: One-line human summary of the failure suitable for inline display.

FailureAction.description: Suggested next step, imperative voice (e.g. "Increase memory allocation").

FailureClassifiedBy.description:
Provenance of the classification: `lookup` = deterministic table hit,
`llm` = produced by the configured LLM provider. Used to render an
AI-assisted disclaimer in the UI and for ops debugging.

ProgressPercent.description:
Approximate task completion percentage derived from HealthOmics ListRunTasks
(or Seqera progress when populated). Denominator grows as the workflow DAG
expands, so prefer showing TasksCompleted/TasksTotal alongside this value.

TasksTotal.description: Total known tasks at last status check (denominator for ProgressPercent).

TasksCompleted.description: Tasks in COMPLETED status at last status check.

TasksRunning.description: Tasks in RUNNING/STARTING status at last status check.

TasksFailed.description: Tasks in FAILED status at last status check.

Effect.description: ALLOW or DENY; omitted/undefined on legacy rows means ALLOW.

UnifiedWorkflowCatalogEntry.description: API / UI catalog row (unified HealthOmics + Seqera).

metadata.description: Any metadata available for workflow. The information listed may vary depending on the workflow, and there may also be no metadata to return.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that's included if more results are available.

WorkflowReport.description:
These are the types for the workflow report endpoint which is not published as part
of the nextflow tower openapi.
